findings.note: requests.get(url) with no timeout and no SSRF guard (scheme/private-IP allowlist) on a fully user-supplied URL
non-functional (undefined requestsheaders, requests.method) so it fails closed; if fixed, uses non-constant-time == HMAC comparison
stores registration password in plaintext, comment notes hashing but never does it
classic SQL injection via f-string interpolation of username (and password) into the query

compares password with row[0]==password, implying plaintext storage/no hashing at rest
redirects to unvalidated next query param, classic open redirect
os.popen string-concatenates unsanitized signature header (command injection) and never actually verifies any signature
zipfile.extractall on user-uploaded archive with no member-path sanitization (zip-slip); also chmod 0o777 on data dir

SQL is parameterized, but password hashed with unsalted single-round SHA-256 (fast hash, no salt)
subprocess.run(command, shell=True) on user-supplied string is direct command injection
SQL is parameterized (safe), but snippet is broken: invalid SQL syntax (WHERELOWER) and truncated incomplete else branch
user-supplied filename used directly in os.path.join with no sanitization, path traversal on upload

user-supplied filename used directly in os.path.join with no sanitization, path traversal on upload
yaml.load() with no Loader argument (unsafe default) on user-supplied YAML, arbitrary object deserialization
code has an indentation/syntax error so it won't execute; if fixed, fallback path skips signature verification for non-JWT payloads and references undefined YOUR_SECRET_KEY
fully parameterized SQL query, no injection
SQL parameterized, but uses Python's built-in non-cryptographic hash() to compare passwords instead of a real password hash
compares password with row[0]==password, implying plaintext storage/no hashing at rest
fully parameterized SQL query, no injection
pickle.load on client-controlled cookie data is unsafe deserialization, potential RCE
stores registration password in plaintext dict, comment acknowledges but doesn't hash
uses yaml.FullLoader instead of safe_load; safer than default Loader but not the recommended safe_load for untrusted input
compares password with row[0]==password, implying plaintext storage/no hashing at rest
SQL injection via f-string interpolation of username into the query

unsalted single-round SHA-256 used for password hashing (fast hash, no salt, no bcrypt/argon2)
session cookie deserialized via base64+json.loads with no signature/integrity check at all, trivially forgeable
